title: Living in Boulder Vlog 3 - 9 - 2017: Black Background

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs about a five-day gap in recording caused by a large file organization and video conversion project, a near-loss of data when a hard drive temporarily failed, and selling his van. He also discusses plans to launch a Patreon page to address money stress, failed a Google AdWords certification exam with 45%, and reflects positively on the test-and-retry format as how education should work.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Never keep anything in only one place, even temporarily — it will break at the worst moment
Retry-after-seven-days testing is how all education should work — engaging and practical
Essentially worthless (~$200/year); Patreon is a better path
Overdue move; wants to do it right with a carefully written introduction page

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I didn't record videos for a few days because I was organizing 350 gigabytes of files.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The files included work, videos of chicks working out, exercises like squatting and deadlifting, and project files.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was converting the files into an organized format, which took a long time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: While converting files, I couldn't record, process, or upload videos because it would crash my computer.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I learned not to store files in only one place, even temporarily, because my external drive failed.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Luckily, the files were recoverable when I took the drive to a computer repair shop.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm selling a van and did some touch-up work like shampooing and fixing the interior.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I lost some paperwork, which might lower the van's selling price.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm stressed about money and decided to start a Patreon to generate income.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My website ads only make about 30 cents a day, which isn't enough.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need to write an introduction for my Patreon page but find it challenging.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might have to sell my motorcycle to make ends meet.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I took an AdWords certification test but only scored 45% and will retake it in seven days.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think the AdWords test format is how education should be—focused on certification and retakes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to study more and pass the AdWords test to improve my website's marketing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'll start recording videos daily now that my file organization is done.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had an issue with a muted video upload due to my computer overheating during file transfers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I restarted my computer to fix the overheating issue during file transfers.

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder, V-Log, for March 9th, Thursday.
I didn't do a V-Log for a few days, because my projects that I've been doing recently,
Organizing everything, or life, have been, let's see,
Oh no, I forgot to pull this out.
I don't want my, too many crinkles.
It's been so many days, I'm not good at, I forget to do certain steps.
Okay, yeah, so what happened?
Well, I wasn't doing it, because my new project was organizing files,
Which I hadn't had organized, it's all over the place.
I had all these files, 350 gigabytes of, like, files of, like, chicks working out,
Like all the different exercises, squatting, deadlifting, all that kind of stuff,
From when I started doing all I wanted to do in my room here.
And even dumbbells, too, and stuff.
And so, and then the CrossFit stuff.
My gym across the street has CrossFit, like, bumper plates and everything.
But anyway, and then bike racing videos, all, everything, all the different distances.
And then project files of stuff that I don't want to talk about, but yeah.
So anyways, I was converting, and I had to organize and then convert all that.
And then, because before they were, like, in the wrong format, and that took forever,
And so while it's converting, I can't record video or process it with, or upload it or anything,
You know what I mean?
And it would take too much of memory.
Because it would crash even if I tried to drag and drop stuff while that converter program was on.
So I just wasn't uploading videos.
But now it's all over.
But then after I did that, I needed to drag that file over,
And then empty stuff on my computer so I could, well, I put it on that,
But I needed to empty stuff on my computer because I consolidated on that.
And so I deleted it, and that was the only place it was in the one hard drive.
And when I tried to plug it up again, it wouldn't plug.
So I had to take it to the computer place, data doctor,
And they're going to go and transfer it to my other hard drive so I can throw that other hard drive away.
But so the lesson is don't ever have stuff backed up only in one place,
Even if it's only intended for, like, five minutes, like what I was planning on doing,
Because it will break then.
So that's my lesson.
But luckily, it plugged up over there, so it was fine.
So anyway, what else is going on in my life?
Oh, yeah, selling the van.
Yeah, I did some touch-up shampooing.
I lost all my paperwork, so I'll probably not have to be able to sell the car for as much.
So today I had to go in and get the title.
And, yeah, I was finishing up, fixing it up.
I put a new rug in underneath.
And, yeah, so tomorrow I'm going to clean it and then vacuum the inside
And then take video and picture and make commercials and stuff to sell the van.
It's super crunch time.
The person over at H&R Block called me, and they're like, hey, let's do taxes.
I'm like, whoa.
So, man, time just comes so fast.
So I'm freaking out about money.
So I decided to go ahead and do a Patreon, get interested in the whole internet marketing thing,
Because I have my website and I have all those ads.
And I make, like, zero pennies to one penny a day on my website, you know?
And I make, like, 30 cents a day or whatever on my YouTube.
I make, like, 200.
It was $230 a year I make with my website and Google and my YouTube.
So it's time I go and start, you know, it's about time I start, yeah, fixing out the service levels of my website,
Which I did, I think, enough, and then make a Patreon page, you know, and see if I can get some, get the money,
See if I can get a little bit of money, because I think I'll have to sell my motorcycle, too.
But, yeah, that's going to be fun, though, because they want you to write the introduction.
And, oh, damn, how the hell am I going to introduce myself in, like, one page or something?
I was looking at other people's Patreon pages, and they have, like, some of them are longer, so.
Yeah.
So, yeah, I'll see if I can blow it down.
I don't want to overdo it.
I've been working on Mind Numbing Week stuff for so long that this is new to me, so it's about time.
But, yeah, I need to, like, concentrate and just take my time on writing something and make it perfect on that website.
And then, yeah, I'll get on all the other websites, too.
And then tomorrow I'll put my car up for sale.
I was a little bit freaked out about that whole losing that file.
I thought I lost all of them, and I was like, I try, I have, I have Carbonite,
But you have to manually click the videos if they're going to back up.
But they took me to some special tool to find the videos, but not all of them over there.
So, luckily they get the people over at the place who can do it.
So, anyways, so that was, like, the last, ooh, five minutes.
That was, like, the last five days.
So, anyways, tomorrow and from here on in I'll do videos every day because I'm done converting.
And, yeah, and I'll talk about my process, my progress with Patreon.
That'll be Patreon.
Oh, and then marketing.
I checked into the marketing because I have the ads, and I'm glad I didn't put too many ads on my website
Because you can put, you should put ads in different ad groups, like a different ad group for a different,
Each page so that you can control that stuff and monitor and everything.
So, yeah, I just attached a bunch of ads on my, like, pre-audio history books or something.
So, yeah, so I took the test.
They have this AdWords test that you give two hours to take 100 questions.
You have to get 80% right.
So I just took the test, and I think the first 10 I just kind of zoomed through just to see what they were.
And I wanted to get screen captures of them.
But then I started taking it seriously because I thought that I was getting them right.
But I ended up getting 45%, so I failed.
You have to get it to the city.
So, yeah, but you get to take it in seven days.
This is what education needs to be.
This is so fun.
This is what school always needed to be, where, you know, if you want to get certified in something,
Whether it's anything, any subject that people go to college for, just do it like they do it here.
The things you have to pass, and you can just wait seven days, and you get two hours, and you do it.
So, yeah, I'll just keep taking it every seven days.
And you do a little bit of studying, have it redo, and then until you pass it.
And then there's the next one, analytics, and then you do that.
And then I'll know, and I'll be able to.
And I have my website, and my YouTube everything, so I have a great thing to work with.
So, yeah, that's what I'm going to go do right now.
And the background will be me talking with my beard.
That was a request.
And I did upload one before that never got up, before my computer started, became tied up.
But it was muted, because the computer was, like, overwhelmed.
It was when my external died, or wouldn't communicate with this computer anymore.
When my computer acts weird, like gets overheated or whatever from transferring to external,
I have to restart it, or else there's no sound when I need the video.
So, anyways, that's why it was like five days.
But that won't happen again.
Okay, peace out.
